presentation_context.teaching_goal: Zweryfikować i wesprzeć wykład o kontraktach FRA: definicje, rozliczenie, wycena stopy FRA, wartość pozycji, przykłady rachunkowe oraz typowe błędy dydaktyczne.

slide_revision_priorities.reason: confidence=medium (+1); extension_relation=extends (+1); corroborated by deep dive window from Fries 2016 (+2); links CL06 and CL03 (high-priority claims)

evidence_refs.quote: Using one curve to both estimating the forward rates and discounting future cash flows is not plausible given the prerequisite of arbitr[age-free pricing]
Forward curve (USD-3M) calibrated from swaps with different collateralization (USD-OIS and EUR-OIS) assuming independence of the market rates

slide_update_plan.finding: Baran & Witzany (2014) and Fries (2016) together confirm that the classical single-curve FRA pricing formula is the correct pedagogical baseline but requires a post-crisis qualifier: forward rate estimation and discounting use separate curves (multi-curve framework) in practice.
slide_update_plan.rationale: Verified by two independent sources in the deep dive. Evidence is bounded to extracted windows; full arbitrage proof not visible but context is unambiguous.

ready_to_apply_text.optional_detail: Corroborated by Fries (2016) Fig.1: USD-3M forward curve calibrated from swaps with USD-OIS vs EUR-OIS collateralization.
ready_to_apply_text.slide_bullet: Uwaga: po 2008 r. wycena instrumentów opartych na stopach IBOR wymaga rozróżnienia krzywej forward i krzywej dyskontowej (podejście multi-curve). Klasyczna formuła FRA pozostaje punktem wyjścia, lecz w praktyce stosuje się korektę o spread bazowy (zob. Baran & Witzany 2014, POLITICKÁ EKONOMIE; Fries 2016 - koroboracja).
ready_to_apply_text.speaker_note: Post-2008 multi-curve framework verifies CL06: single-curve FRA formula is pedagogically correct but needs a caveat for real-market practice where IBOR forward curve ≠ OIS discount curve. Baran & Witzany (2014) confirm basis spread presence; Fries (2016) shows calibration of separate OIS and IBOR forward curves.

unresolved_items.question: No source in Scout corpus addresses FRA settlement discounting formula (CL05: payment discounted to T1). TOPIC_FRA_SETTLEMENT_DAY_COUNT_EXAMPLES had only off-topic downloads (mental health day-care, cosmology).
unresolved_items.recommendation: New Scout run targeting FRA settlement formula textbooks (Hull, McDonald) or BBA FRA documentation required.

unresolved_items.question: No source addresses the FRA timeline pedagogy (T0/T1/T2 stage separation). TOPIC_FRA_TIMELINE_PEDAGOGY found only a physical education pedagogy paper.
unresolved_items.recommendation: Targeted re-run with FRA-specific keyword anchors (FRA timeline, settlement date, reset date, maturity date).

unresolved_items.question: Multi-curve FRA pricing: no source provides explicit FRA no-arbitrage formula under multi-curve (deep dive windows truncated or abstract only).
unresolved_items.recommendation: Full-text access to Fries (2016) chapter would resolve; alternatively cite Bianchetti (2010) on single-curve vs multi-curve FRA.

unresolved_items.question: Smith-Wilson term-structure model (SCOUT_A343DAEE69ED1A50B124): A07 classified as context_only. Deep dive windows contain copyright page + NS/Svensson discussion. No FRA formula.
Commercial bank IR risk paper (Wright & Houpt 1996): A07 context_only. Deep dive p11 discusses bank gap analysis, not FRA formula.
Czech Crown money market (Staniek 2016): A07 context_only for FRA settlement. Deep dive confirms PRIBOR-based pricing context only, no settlement formula.
Zhao & Moser (2017) Bank Lending and IR Derivatives: A07 context_only. Windows show regression methodology; no FRA payoff or OTC settlement mechanics.
Darby (1994) OTC Derivatives and Systemic Risk: A07 context_only. 1994 historical paper on OTC market structure; no FRA cash settlement formula.
Pepic (2014) Managing IR Risk with Futures: deep dive p2 confirms paper is about IR futures, not FRA OTC cash settlement.
Drees & Nguyen (2001) Modern Banking and OTC Derivatives: A07 context_only. Windows provide institutional OTC market context; no FRA long/short payoff formula.
